items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help shopping center businesses get licensed without penalties. This could affect how local retail and small businesses operate in the city's commercial centers.

items.headline: New economic incentives coming for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council approved new incentive programs aimed at attracting and supporting retail shops and startups in Cupertino. This could influence what kinds of businesses locate here and how vibrant local shopping areas become.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the March 24 meeting. This brings new housing to the city and was significant enough to require a second reading by council.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program Round 4 at the March 24 meeting. This state grant could fund local efforts to streamline housing development and reduce barriers to building.

items.headline: City seeks public input on how to spend HUD housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on its draft plan for spending federal housing funds in fiscal year 2026-27. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the city finalizes the spending plan.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program likely addresses vehicle dwelling and homelessness in the city.
